content: @Miguel Ojeda I was thinking that is maybe better to have also rust-dev added in KernelCI as most development is done there :thinking:

by the way I have replay to your question, if you have any other question feel free to ask
https://github.com/kernelci/kernelci-core/pull/2221#issuecomment-1861904143

Thanks!

If @Boqun Feng is OK with that and KernelCI has the resources for it, then it would be definitely great!

(Note that rust-dev may take Rust version upgrades sooner, so we will have some extra failures in either side, but that is OK)

Thanks ;-) I think it's OK as long as KernelCI has the resources. One thing to notice though is that rust-dev is supposed to be "unstable" (or maybe "broken"): I sometimes pick patches that are still in early stages of development to see how they play along with other patches and also sometimes I need to resolve conflicts between patchsets, but I may make mistakes. And also what Miguel mentioned about Rust version upgrades.
